expectations.text: Output includes standard .kql comment header block
expectations.evidence: File opens with '// ===...===' block containing Title, Service: Sentinel, Tables: Syslog, Description (noting translation from Splunk SPL), Parameters (lookbackWindow, failureThreshold, uniqueSourceMin, successWindow), Complexity: Intermediate

expectations.text: Uses Syslog table for Linux authentication logs
expectations.evidence: Both failedAttempts and successfulLogins subqueries use 'Syslog' table with 'Facility == "auth" or ProcessName has "sshd"'

expectations.text: Maps Splunk dc() to KQL dcount()
expectations.evidence: 'UniqueSources = dcount(SourceIP)' with inline comment '// dcount() == dc() in SPL'

expectations.text: Maps Splunk values() to KQL make_set()
expectations.evidence: 'Sources = make_set(SourceIP)' with inline comment '// make_set() == values() in SPL — unique IPs as array'

expectations.text: Uses join to correlate failed logins with successful logins by user
expectations.evidence: 'failedAttempts | join kind=inner successfulLogins on TargetUser' — inner join correlates users with both failure and success records

expectations.text: Uses iff() or case() instead of Splunk eval/if syntax
expectations.evidence: 'SuccessAfterBruteForce = iff(LastSuccess > ago(successWindow), "YES", "NO")' with comment '// Equivalent to: eval success_after_bruteforce=if(...)'
'| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookbackWindow)' in both failedAttempts and successfulLogins subqueries

claims.claim: Uses regex extract to parse TargetUser and SourceIP from SyslogMessage
claims.evidence: extract(@"Failed password for (?:invalid user )?(\ S+) from", 1, SyslogMessage) handles both 'Failed password for user' and 'Failed password for invalid user' patterns

claims.claim: Documents SPL-to-KQL mapping with inline comments throughout
claims.evidence: Every significant aggregation has an SPL equivalent comment, e.g., '// Equivalent to: stats count as failures dc(src_ip) as unique_sources values(src_ip) as sources by user'

eval_feedback.overall: All 7 assertions pass. The inline SPL mapping comments are thorough and add significant value. No suggestions.
